name: Joshua Herrington

podcast.title: Leveraging Military Fortitude in Financial Services
podcast.meta: Nov 27, 2024 · 52 min · with Chris Gandy & Suzanne Carawan

testimonial.pull: Joshua is the epitome of the word humble — a reliable, helpful, and humble servant leader.
body: I looked up the definition of humble, and in my experience working with Joshua, he is the epitome of the word. Joshua has always been available, always been knowledgeable, and guided me with assisting clients better.

He has often shared how proud he is of his family, his friends, and his coworkers. He often speaks to how he wants to help the people in his life prevail in their own lives. I have often been surprised at many of Joshua's personal and professional accomplishments, as he humbly does not share them unless asked. He chooses to focus on how he can assist others.

Many of you may know that Joshua is a veteran and served in the U.S. Air Force. The armed forces teach the concept of servant leadership — a philosophy where a leader's primary goal is to serve and empower others, rather than accumulate personal power.

With my own background in the U.S. Army, as a public-safety member and in financial services, I have been careful who I recommend. In my entire career this is only the 3rd recommendation I have written — and each I requested to provide. Joshua did not ask me; I asked him if I could. I believe Joshua has proven himself to be a reliable, helpful, and humble servant leader.
testimonial.name: Scott Bovee
testimonial.context: U.S. Army veteran · public safety & financial services

hero.sub: Court of the Table advisor · compulsive builder · always drafting the next chapter.
hero.caption: Kyrgyzstan — the three of us

mission.heading: A check. Not a maybe.
mission.body1: My work is protecting Americans — the middle class, the mass affluent, the next-door millionaire — with guarantees and risk-based planning. On the worst day of someone's life, the diagnosis or the breadwinner who doesn't come home, I want to show up with a check. Not a market-dependent figure. A check.
mission.body2: A man of two worlds: I served the world — Japan, Europe, Canada — and came home to protect the people who build this country. The American taxpayer, the mass-affluent resident, the expat and the immigrant whose money is American-taxed. If it protects a family, that's my front.
